code: function set_vars() { $this->del = chr(127); $this->pattern = '(^|href=\"|src=\"|srcset=\"|data-link=\"|[0-9]+w, )'; $this->wpurl = untrailingslashit( get_bloginfo( 'wpurl' ) ); $this->url = untrailingslashit( get_bloginfo( 'url' ) ); $this->wpurl = apply_filters( 'of_absolute_relative_urls_wpurl', $this->wpurl, $this->url ); $this->url = apply_filters( 'of_absolute_relative_urls_url', $this->url, $this->wpurl ); rsort( $urls ); $wp_upload = wp_upload_dir(); ... }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Uses tabs for indentation per WP coding standards. However, chr(127) for delete character is unconventional; get_bloginfo('wpurl') is deprecated in favor of get_bloginfo('url', 'raw').

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL queries present. Pure string manipulation and URL processing.

security.analysis: Uses get_bloginfo which returns sanitized output. apply_filters allows extensibility. No direct user input handling in this method. The regex pattern uses backtick escaping that could be fragile across PHP versions.

performance.analysis: O(n) where n is number of URLs. rsort is efficient. wp_upload_dir() call is cached internally by WP. Filter chains are reasonable.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correctly uses untrailingslashit, apply_filters, wp_upload_dir, get_bloginfo. Proper WP hooks pattern for extensibility.

code_quality.analysis: Well-structured initialization method. Good use of filters for extensibility. Missing PHPDoc block.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Uses core WP functions exclusively. No external dependencies required.

i18n.analysis: No user-facing strings. All text is structural. No i18n concerns.

accessibility.analysis: Not directly related to accessibility. URL handling is neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Clean URL normalization with proper WP filter hooks for extensibility.

code: function set_filters() { $view_filters = array('the_editor_content','the_content','get_the_excerpt'); $save_filters = array('content_save_pre','excerpt_save_pre'); $option_filters = apply_filters( 'of_absolute_relative_urls_option_filters', $option_filters ); foreach( $option_filters as $filter ) { add_filter( 'option_' . $filter, array( $this, 'absolute_url' ) ); add_filter( 'pre_update_option_' . $filter, array( $this, 'relative_url' ) ); } }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Standard WP filter registration pattern. Tab indentation present. Hardcoded filter hook names are correct WP conventions.

security.analysis: Hook names are hardcoded and validated internally. No user input. The dynamic filter registration is safe because filter names are constructed from known-safe strings.

performance.analysis: Adds multiple filters dynamically. Each filter callback will be invoked on every the_content/the_editor_content call which could accumulate overhead.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct use of add_filter, apply_filters, get_option('template'), get_option('stylesheet'). Proper WP hook ecosystem integration.

code_quality.analysis: Clear organization of view vs save filters. The hardcoded widget plugin slugs (black-studio-tinymce, sow-editor) are fragile.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Hardcodes other plugin slugs in filter names which creates implicit coupling to specific plugins.

accessibility.analysis: Indirectly improves accessibility by handling editor content URLs correctly.

reasoning.key_observation: Robust filter registration but fragile hardcoded plugin slug dependencies.

code: function filter_post_content( $filter = false, $post = '' ) { if ( $filter ) { if ( empty( $post ) ) { global $post; } $post->post_content = $this->absolute_url( $post->post_content ); $post->post_excerpt = $this->absolute_url( $post->post_excerpt ); wp_cache_replace( $post->ID, $post, 'posts' ); } return $filter; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Uses tabs. Function signature follows WP convention. Missing type hints but WP doesn't enforce them.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. Cache operation is the closest to data persistence.

security.analysis: Modifies $post object directly which bypasses some sanitization. wp_cache_replace without $group parameter is deprecated. Could leave post object in inconsistent state if cache fail.

performance.analysis: wp_cache_replace call without TTL or proper cache group ($group parameter) may fail silently or corrupt cache. This is a known WP cache API misuse.

wp_api_usage.analysis: wp_cache_replace() requires 4th $group parameter. Missing it defaults to 'posts' but triggers deprecation notice in WP 4.9+. Should use wp_cache_set() instead for clarity.

code_quality.analysis: Global $post fallback is a code smell. Should use wp_to_global() pattern. The early return on empty $post after setting global is logically confusing.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Uses core WP cache API and post object access.

accessibility.analysis: Content filtering is neutral to accessibility.

reasoning.key_observation: Correct intent but wp_cache_replace misuse without $group parameter is a WP API regression bug.

code: function run_acf_page_builder() { require_once( plugin_dir_path(__FILE__) . '/includes/class.acf_page_builder.php' ); require_once( plugin_dir_path(__FILE__) . '/admin/functions.php' ); require_once( plugin_dir_path(__FILE__) . '/public/functions.php' ); }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Standard require_once pattern. plugin_dir_path() is correct WP convention.

security.analysis: require_once is safe. No user input involved.

performance.analysis: Minimal overhead, just file includes.

wp_api_usage.analysis: plugin_dir_path() is the correct WP API for plugin-relative paths.

code_quality.analysis: Simple and clear. Could be consolidated into an autoloader.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Self-contained plugin dependencies.

accessibility.analysis: Neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Simple, correct plugin bootstrap with standard WP path resolution.

code: function get_sections( $id = '' ) { global $post; if( !$id && isset($post) ) $id = $post->ID; if( !$id ) return false; $pb = new ACF_Page_Builder(); $pb->addFieldsToWP(); $html = $pb->getSectionsHTML( 'acf_page_builder', (string) $id ); return isset($html) ? $html : ''; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Tab indentation. Missing PHPDoc. Short-circuit return is fine.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. Relies on ACF's internal data access.

security.analysis: Post ID cast to string mitigates injection. No direct user input but $id parameter is unchecked type.

performance.analysis: Instantiates new ACF_Page_Builder every call which may be wasteful. Could be cached.

wp_api_usage.analysis: global $post pattern is standard WP. (string) cast is good practice.

code_quality.analysis: Clean public function pattern. isset($html) check is defensive but redundant given the constructor.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on ACF being loaded first.

accessibility.analysis: Neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Standard shortcode/helper pattern with proper $post fallback.

code: function the_sections( $id = '' ) { $html = get_sections( $id ); echo $html; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Follows WP convention of the_ prefix for echo functions. But echo should use esc_html or wp_kses_post for output sanitization.

security.analysis: Direct echo of HTML without wp_kses_post or esc_html is a XSS vulnerability if get_sections returns untrusted content.

performance.analysis: Minimal, just delegates to get_sections.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Standard the_() wrapper pattern (like the_content).

code_quality.analysis: Overly trivial. Could use inline function or just document that echo happens.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on get_sections.

accessibility.analysis: Neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Wp_kses_post missing on echo output - XSS risk with unfiltered HTML.

code: function acfpg_init_public() { if( is_admin() ) return false; $pb = new ACF_Page_Builder(); add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', array( $pb, 'enqueuePublic') ); }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Tab indentation. is_admin() check is standard. Missing return type.

security.analysis: is_admin() gate is correct for separating admin/public logic.

performance.analysis: Early return for admin requests avoids unnecessary object creation.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct use of add_action with wp_enqueue_scripts hook. is_admin() is proper WP function.

code_quality.analysis: Clean separation. Should use add_action('init', 'acfpg_init_public', 1) for proper hook timing.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Self-contained.

accessibility.analysis: Neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Correct public/admin separation with proper WP enqueue hook.

code: function __construct() { include_once( ABSPATH . 'wp-admin/includes/plugin.php' ); $this->acf_active = is_plugin_active( 'advanced-custom-fields/acf.php' ) || is_plugin_active( 'advanced-custom-fields-pro/acf.php' ); $this->scf_active = is_plugin_active( 'simple-contact-forms/simple-contact-forms.php' ); $this->supported_formats = array('post_type', 'page_template'); $this->use_bs = function_exists('get_field') ? get_field('acfpb_use_bootstrap') : false; }

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. is_plugin_active queries option tables internally.

security.analysis: function_exists('get_field') check prevents fatal error. No user input exposure.

performance.analysis: is_plugin_active() does get_option on every call which is somewhat expensive. Could cache the result.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct use of is_plugin_active, function_exists, get_field. ABSPATH usage is standard.

code_quality.analysis: Constructor side-effect of calling get_field() is unusual. Should separate detection from initialization.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Hardcodes ACF and SCF plugin slugs as implicit dependencies.

accessibility.analysis: Neutral.

reasoning.key_observation: Standard plugin dependency detection but constructor should not call get_field().

code: function enqueuePublic() { wp_enqueue_style( 'acfpb-public', plugin_dir_url( __FILE__ ) . '../public/css/acfpb_styles.css', false, NULL, 'all' ); }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Standard enqueue pattern. Tab indentation consistent.

security.analysis: plugin_dir_url() is safe. No user input in CSS path.

performance.analysis: Single CSS enqueue. Minimal overhead.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct use of wp_enqueue_style with dependencies array (false) and media param ('all'). NULL version is deprecated, should use filemtime or a hardcoded version.

code_quality.analysis: Simple and correct. NULL version parameter should be replaced with a version string or filemtime.

dependency_integrity.analysis: No external deps beyond WP core.

accessibility.analysis: CSS enqueue is neutral to accessibility.

reasoning.key_observation: Correct CSS enqueue with deprecated NULL version parameter.

security.analysis: Empty function, no risk.

performance.analysis: No-op, zero cost.

code_quality.analysis: Empty method should have a comment explaining why it's empty or be removed if not needed.

accessibility.analysis: N/A.

code: function getSectionsHTML( $name, $post_id ) { if( !$this->acf_active ) return ''; if( have_rows( $name, $post_id ) ): $s = 1; $this->html = '<div id="acfpb_sections">'; while ( have_rows( $name, $post_id ) ) : the_row(); ... $this->html .= '<div id="' . $id . '" class="' . $class . '" style="' . $style . '">'; endif; endwhile; return $this->html; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Tab indentation. PHPDoc missing. String concatenation with . is correct WP style.

sql_safety.analysis: No direct SQL. Uses ACF's have_rows/the_row which handle DB access.

security.analysis: Major issue: $style from get_sub_field('bg') is used directly in inline style attribute without sanitization. $wrapper class from get_sub_field is concatenated without class sanitization. $id is built from counter but still should be sanitized.

performance.analysis: String concatenation in loop ($this->html .= ...) causes O(n^2) memory copies. Should use output buffering or array join pattern.

wp_api_usage.analysis: have_rows/the_row/get_row_layout/get_sub_field are ACF API, not WP core. This is acceptable for an ACF plugin.

code_quality.analysis: Deeply nested HTML generation is hard to read. $this->html property mutation is a side-effect. Multiple endif/endwhile without indentation levels is fragile.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on ACF fields being configured correctly.

accessibility.analysis: Generated HTML uses Bootstrap classes without ARIA roles or semantic elements. img tags lack alt attributes. Layout structure has no role attributes.

reasoning.key_observation: HTML generation with multiple security gaps: unsanitized style/class injection and missing image alt attributes.

performance.analysis: Single section render, reasonable performance.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct ACF field retrieval pattern.

code_quality.analysis: HTML mixed with PHP logic without templating. Missing alt attribute on img tag.

code: function getSection_button() { $class = get_sub_field('button_class'); $text = get_sub_field('button_text'); switch ( get_sub_field('link_to_page') ) { case 'anchor': $href = '#' . get_sub_field('button_anchor'); break; case 'external': $href = get_sub_field('external_link'); break; case 'internal': $href = get_sub_field('internal_link'); break; default: $href = '#'; break; } $html = '' . $text . ''; return $html; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Standard PHP patterns.

performance.analysis: Simple render, fine.

wp_api_usage.analysis: ACF field API usage correct.

code_quality.analysis: Clear switch pattern. Could use a map for cleaner structure.

dependency_integrity.analysis: ACF-dependent.

accessibility.analysis: Anchor link is semantic but missing rel attributes for external links (noopener noreferrer).

reasoning.key_observation: No esc_url on href, no wp_kses on text, no sanitize_html_class on class - full unsanitized data flow to HTML.

performance.analysis: Linear iteration, fine.

code_quality.analysis: Clean foreach pattern but string concat in loop.

code: function getSection_raw_html() { $html = get_sub_field('html'); return $html; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Simple function.

security.analysis: Returns raw HTML which caller will render. The function itself is neutral but the name 'raw_html' implies intentional unfiltered output. Caller must handle sanitization.

performance.analysis: Minimal.

wp_api_usage.analysis: ACF API.

code_quality.analysis: Clear intent despite the name. Should be documented as intentional passthrough.

dependency_integrity.analysis: ACF-dependent.

accessibility.analysis: Returns whatever HTML ACF provides, so accessibility depends on input.

reasoning.key_observation: Intentional raw HTML passthrough - safe as a utility but caller must handle output sanitization.

code: function getSection_simple_contact_forms() { if( !function_exists('simple_contact_form') ) return ''; $options = array(); if( get_sub_field('form_title') !== '' ) $options['form_title'] = get_sub_field('form_title'); if( get_sub_field('button') == true ) { $options['button'] = true; $options['form_collapsed'] = true; } $html = simple_contact_form( $options ); return $html; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: function_exists check is good WP practice.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. Delegates to another plugin.

security.analysis: function_exists is good defense-in-depth. Options passed to simple_contact_form are constructed from ACF fields which are untrusted. Should validate option values before passing.

performance.analysis: Delegates to external function, minimal overhead.

wp_api_usage.analysis: function_exists is correct WP pattern for optional dependency detection.

code_quality.analysis: Clear optional dependency pattern. Could be more defensive with is_array() checks on get_sub_field results.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Hardcoded dependency on 'simple-contact-forms' plugin without explicit plugin header dependency.

accessibility.analysis: Depends on the contact form plugin's accessibility.

reasoning.key_observation: Correct optional dependency pattern but should validate ACF field values before passing to external function.

wp_api_usage.analysis: ACF API correct.

code_quality.analysis: Minimal function, clear intent.

code: function acfpg_init_admin() { if( !is_admin() ) return false; $pb = new ACF_Page_Builder(); add_action('wp_enqueue_script', array( $pb, 'enqueueAdmin') ); $pb->addFieldsToWP(); }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: is_admin() check standard.

security.analysis: is_admin() gate provides basic access control but is_admin() can be bypassed in REST API contexts. Missing capability check.

performance.analysis: Instantiates ACF_Page_Builder on every admin page load regardless of need. Should be on admin_init hook.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Critical bug: add_action('wp_enqueue_script', ...) should be add_action('admin_enqueue_scripts', ...). 'wp_enqueue_script' is an action, not a hook for enqueuing. This will never fire correctly.

code_quality.analysis: Hook name 'wp_enqueue_script' is wrong - that's a function, not an action. Should be 'admin_enqueue_scripts'.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Self-contained.

accessibility.analysis: Admin UI depends on proper CSS/JS loading.

reasoning.key_observation: Wrong hook name 'wp_enqueue_script' instead of 'admin_enqueue_scripts' means admin assets never load.

code: function save() { current_user_can( ADS_TXT_MANAGE_CAPABILITY ) || die; check_admin_referer( 'adstxt_save' ); $_post = stripslashes_deep( $_POST ); $lines = preg_split( '/\r\n|\r|\n/', $_post['adstxt'] ); $sanitized = array(); $errors = array(); foreach ( $lines as $i => $line ) { $result = validate_line( $line, $line_number, $has_only_placeholder_records ); $sanitized[] = $result['sanitized']; ... } $postarr = array('ID' => $post_id, 'post_title' => 'Ads.txt', 'post_content' => $sanitized, 'post_type' => 'adstxt', 'post_status' => 'publish'); $post_id = wp_insert_post( $postarr ); wp_safe_redirect( esc_url_raw( $_post['_wp_http_referer'] ) . '&updated=true' ); exit; }

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Good WP coding patterns: current_user_can, check_admin_referer, wp_insert_post, wp_safe_redirect, esc_url_raw. All security fundamentals present.

sql_safety.analysis: No direct SQL. wp_insert_post handles DB safely with $wpdb prepare internally.

security.analysis: Excellent: current_user_can check, check_admin_referer nonce verification, stripslashes_deep for input sanitization, wp_safe_redirect, esc_url_raw. validate_line() provides content validation. Minor: $_POST accessed without isset guards on all keys.

performance.analysis: preg_split on each save is reasonable. validate_line per line is O(n*m). wp_insert_post adds DB overhead but is necessary.

wp_api_usage.analysis: Correct use of wp_insert_post, wp_safe_redirect, esc_url_raw, current_user_can, check_admin_referer, stripslashes_deep. Proper WP security API integration.

code_quality.analysis: Comprehensive save flow with validation, error collection, and warning handling. Good separation of concerns. Could benefit from extracting validate_line logic.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Requires validate_line function and ADS_TXT_MANAGE_CAPABILITY constant.

i18n.analysis: Errors and warnings arrays suggest i18n-ready messages. Only hardcoded 'Ads.txt' and 'App-ads.txt' titles.

accessibility.analysis: Admin functionality, accessibility depends on the admin UI wrapper.

reasoning.key_observation: Well-secured save function with nonce, capability check, and content validation - the gold standard for WP admin handlers.
